steps.title: Find the decay factor
steps.content:
A decrease of $24\%$ means $76\%$ of the mass remains after each $22.96$-minute period:
$$
1-0.24=0.76
$$

steps.title: Write the exponent
steps.content:
The number of $22.96$-minute periods in $t$ minutes is
$$
\frac{t}{22.96}
$$
So the model must raise the decay factor $0.76$ to that exponent.

steps.title: Match the model
steps.content: The equation needs initial mass $100$, decay factor $0.76$, and exponent $$ \frac{t}{22.96} $$ The answer is D.
